content.problemStatement:
Chef and his son Chefu want to watch a match of their favourite football team playing against another team, which will take place today.

Unfortunately, Chef is busy in the kitchen preparing lunch, so he cannot watch the match. Therefore, every now and then, Chef asks Chefu about the current scores of the teams and Chefu tells him the score. However, Chefu sometimes does not specify the teams along with the score — for example, he could say "the score is 3 to 6", and there is no way to know just from this statement if it was their favourite team or the other team that scored 6 goals; in different statements, the order of teams may also be different. Other times, Chefu specifies which team scored how many goals.

Chef asks for the score and receives a reply NNN times. There are two types of replies:


`1 A B`: their favourite team scored AAA goals and the other team scored BBB goals
`2 A B`: one team scored AAA goals and the other scored BBB goals
Chef is asking you to help him determine the score of his favourite team after each of Chefu's replies. After each reply, based on the current reply and all previous information (but without any following replies), tell him whether it is possible to certainly determine the number of goals scored by his favourite team.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
there is at least one valid sequence of scored goals consistent with all replies
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The following NNN lines describe Chefu's replies in the format described above.
content.outputFormat: For each of Chefu's replies, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to determine the score of Chef's favourite team after this reply or `"NO"` if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.output:
NO
YES
YES
NO
YES
NO
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

Reply 1: Chef cannot know who scored the first goal.
Reply 2: Chefu told Chef that their favourite team has scored 333 goals so far.
Reply 3: Chef can conclude that his favourite team has scored 444 goals, since it already scored 333 goals earlier.
Reply 4: the favourite team could have scored 555 or 666 goals, but there is no way to know which option is correct.
Reply 5: since there is a tie, Chef knows that his favourite team has scored 888 goals.
Reply 6: again, Chef cannot know if his favourite team has scored 999 or 101010 goals.

hidden.expectedOutput:
NO
YES
YES
NO
YES
NO
